label: Graph neural networks

node.label: Graph neural networks
node.definition: A graph neural network repeatedly aggregates messages from neighboring nodes or edges to learn representations that respect relational structure.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Graph neural networks is modeled as depending on Message passing & neighborhood aggregation, Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data and feeding downstream nodes such as Graph transformers, Drug discovery, protein & molecular design, Computer vision, perception & scene understanding.
examples: atlas downstream link to Graph transformers
atlas downstream link to Drug discovery, protein & molecular design
atlas downstream link to Computer vision, perception & scene understanding
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Message passing & neighborhood aggregation, Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data.

dependencies.rationale: Graph neural networks is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Message passing & neighborhood aggregation.
Graph neural networks consumes, emits, or organizes information using Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data.
In this atlas graph, Graph neural networks has a `composes` dependency on Pooling & local aggregation with `common` strength.
In this atlas graph, Graph neural networks has a `composes` dependency on Equivariance, invariance & geometric priors with `common` strength.
